source_text: I think Maria talks too much.

reference_translations.text: Penso che Maria parli troppo.
reference_translations.notes: Standard. penso che triggers the present subjunctive; parlare singular subjunctive is parli.

reference_translations.text: Credo che Maria parli troppo.
reference_translations.notes: credo che is an equivalent opinion trigger.

reference_translations.text: Penso che Maria parla troppo.
reference_translations.notes: WRONG: indicative parla after penso che. Guards the indicative-instead-of-subjunctive miss.

explanation: After an opinion verb (penso che, credo che) Italian uses the present subjunctive in the che-clause. parlare takes -i in the singular: parli, not the indicative parla.

source_text: It's important that you all work harder.

reference_translations.text: È importante che voi lavoriate di più.
reference_translations.notes: Standard. è importante che triggers the subjunctive; the voi -are subjunctive is -iate: lavoriate.

reference_translations.text: È importante che lavoriate di più.
reference_translations.notes: Subject pronoun voi dropped; the verb ending already marks the person.

explanation: The voi present subjunctive is -iate for every conjugation: lavoriate, distinct from the indicative lavorate. The impersonal è importante che requires the subjunctive.

source_text: I hope they leave early tomorrow.

reference_translations.text: Spero che partano presto domani.
reference_translations.notes: Standard. partire takes -ano in the 3rd-person plural subjunctive: partano, not the indicative partono.

reference_translations.text: Spero che se ne vadano presto domani.
reference_translations.notes: Alternative with andarsene; valid but shifts the verb. The intended target is partano.

explanation: spero che triggers the subjunctive. The -ere/-ire 3rd-person plural subjunctive is -ano: partano. The indicative partono is the common slip.

source_text: I doubt the shop opens on Sundays.

reference_translations.text: Dubito che il negozio apra la domenica.
reference_translations.notes: Standard. dubito che triggers the subjunctive; aprire is a plain -ire verb, so the 3sg is apra.

reference_translations.text: Dubito che il negozio sia aperto la domenica.
reference_translations.notes: Alternative using essere aperto (to be open) rather than the verb aprire; both natural.

explanation: dubito che (doubt) requires the subjunctive. aprire is a plain -ire verb with no -isc-, so the 3rd-person singular subjunctive is apra, not the indicative apre.

source_text: I don't think he understands the problem.

reference_translations.text: Non credo che capisca il problema.
reference_translations.notes: Standard, subject pronoun dropped. capire is an -isc- verb: capisca in the singular subjunctive.

reference_translations.text: Non credo che lui capisca il problema.
reference_translations.notes: Subject pronoun lui included for emphasis or clarity.

reference_translations.text: Non credo che capisce il problema.
reference_translations.notes: WRONG: indicative capisce after non credo che. Guards the indicative miss.

explanation: capire belongs to the -isc- group, which inserts -isc- in the singular: capisca. non credo che (negated opinion) strongly favours the subjunctive.

source_text: It's necessary that you all finish the work today.

reference_translations.text: Bisogna che voi finiate il lavoro oggi.
reference_translations.notes: Standard. The voi subjunctive of an -isc- verb drops the -isc-: finiate, not finisciate.

reference_translations.text: Bisogna che finiate il lavoro oggi.
reference_translations.notes: Subject pronoun voi dropped.

explanation: The -isc- insert appears in the singular and 3rd-person plural but never in noi/voi. The voi subjunctive of finire is finiate; finisciate (carrying the insert in) is the over-extension to avoid.

source_text: I want you to look for a solution.

reference_translations.text: Voglio che tu cerchi una soluzione.
reference_translations.notes: Standard. cercare (-care) inserts an h before the subjunctive -i: cerchi.

reference_translations.text: Voglio che cerchi una soluzione.
reference_translations.notes: Subject pronoun tu dropped; the cerchi ending marks the person.

translation.reveal: to look for / to search - cercare

explanation: English 'want someone to do' becomes voglio che + subjunctive in Italian. cercare ends in -care, so it inserts an h to keep the hard c before the -i ending: cerchi.

source_text: It's only right that they pay the bill.

reference_translations.text: È giusto che paghino il conto.
reference_translations.notes: Standard. pagare (-gare) inserts an h: paghino in the 3rd-person plural subjunctive.

reference_translations.text: È giusto che siano loro a pagare il conto.
reference_translations.notes: Cleft alternative emphasising 'they'; valid but restructures. The intended target is paghino.

explanation: è giusto che is an impersonal evaluation trigger requiring the subjunctive. pagare ends in -gare, so it adds an h before the -ino ending: paghino, not pagano (indicative) or pagino (missing h).

source_text: I hope Marco comes to the party tonight.

reference_translations.text: Spero che Marco venga alla festa stasera.
reference_translations.notes: Standard. venire builds its subjunctive from vengo: venga.

reference_translations.text: Spero che Marco venga alla festa questa sera.
reference_translations.notes: questa sera for stasera; equivalent.

reference_translations.text: Spero che Marco viene alla festa stasera.
reference_translations.notes: WRONG: indicative viene after spero che. Guards the indicative miss.

explanation: This irregular group builds the present subjunctive from the io indicative: vengo gives venga. spero che requires the subjunctive, ruling out the indicative viene.

source_text: I think they are all leaving tonight.

reference_translations.text: Penso che vadano via tutti stasera.
reference_translations.notes: Standard. andare builds its subjunctive from vado: 3rd-person plural vadano.

reference_translations.text: Penso che se ne vadano tutti stasera.
reference_translations.notes: With andarsene; natural and idiomatic.

translation.reveal: to leave / to go away - andare via

explanation: andare builds the subjunctive from vado: the 3rd-person plural is vadano, not the indicative vanno. penso che triggers the subjunctive.

source_text: I want you to pay attention in class.

reference_translations.text: Voglio che tu faccia attenzione in classe.
reference_translations.notes: Standard. fare builds its subjunctive from faccio: faccia.

reference_translations.text: Voglio che faccia attenzione in classe.
reference_translations.notes: Subject pronoun tu dropped.

explanation: 'pay attention' is fare attenzione in Italian. fare builds the subjunctive from faccio: faccia. voglio che requires the subjunctive.

source_text: I think it is a good idea.

reference_translations.text: Penso che sia una buona idea.
reference_translations.notes: Standard. essere has the suppletive subjunctive sia.

reference_translations.text: Credo che sia una buona idea.
reference_translations.notes: credo che, an equivalent trigger.

reference_translations.text: Penso che è una buona idea.
reference_translations.notes: WRONG: indicative è after penso che. Guards the very common essere indicative miss.

explanation: essere has its own subjunctive, sia, not derivable from any other form. After penso che it replaces the indicative è. This is the highest-frequency subjunctive form to master.

source_text: I don't believe he is right.

reference_translations.text: Non credo che abbia ragione.
reference_translations.notes: Standard, subject pronoun dropped. avere ragione = to be right; subjunctive of avere is abbia.

reference_translations.text: Non credo che lui abbia ragione.
reference_translations.notes: Subject pronoun lui included.

explanation: Italian says avere ragione (to have reason) for 'to be right'. avere has the suppletive subjunctive abbia, with a double b and no h. non credo che favours the subjunctive over the indicative ha.

source_text: I hope your father is feeling better now.

reference_translations.text: Spero che tuo padre stia meglio adesso.
reference_translations.notes: Standard. 'to feel (well/unwell)' is stare; the subjunctive of stare is stia.

reference_translations.text: Spero che tuo padre stia meglio ora.
reference_translations.notes: ora for adesso; equivalent.

explanation: Italian uses stare for physical/health states: sta meglio = is feeling better. The subjunctive of stare is stia; spero che requires it over the indicative sta.
